en.name: Monocytes in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Monocytes are circulating mononuclear phagocytes (typically 2–10 % of leukocytes) that differentiate into tissue macrophages and dendritic cells [1]. The absolute monocyte count is part of the CBC differential and is used to assess chronic inflammation, infection, and haematological disorders (notably chronic myelomonocytic leukaemia, where monocytes are persistently elevated). The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a haematology analyser as part of the differential white-cell count, on whole blood (EDTA tube). It is collected in routine biochemistry and intensive care. Results are reported as an absolute number concentration, typically in **`10*9/L`** (NPU SI convention, equivalent to `G/L`) or in the US notation **`10*3/uL`** (numerically identical: `1 ×10⁹/L = 1 ×10³/µL`). ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on the LOINC Group `40654069 / LG32885-2 — Monocytes|Number Concentration (count/vol)|Moment in time|Blood (LOINC Group)` and pulls in all its descendants. This resolves to four standard concepts, which differ by counting method. ### Generic (method not specified) - *3001604 / 26484-6 — Monocytes [#/volume] in Blood (LOINC)*: most widely used LOINC code, returned by haematology analysers when the method is not detailed. ### Method-specific - *3033575 / 742-7 — Monocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Automated count (LOINC)*: automated impedance, optical or flow-cytometric count (most common in routine practice). - *3034107 / 743-5 — Monocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Manual count (LOINC)*: manual differential on a stained smear under microscope. - *1092234 / 104548-3 — Monocytes [#/volume] in Blood by Flow cytometry (FC) (LOINC)*: dedicated flow-cytometry assay (e.g. for CMML diagnostic workup, where monocyte subsets are quantified). Recently introduced LOINC code; rare in routine data. **Note on units.** Recommended unit: **`10*9/L`** (`9444 / 10*9/L — billion per liter (UCUM)`), aligned with NPU [2]. LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS` is `10*3/uL` (`8848 / 10*3/uL — thousand per microliter (UCUM)`), which is the same physical quantity in US notation. Conversion: `1 ×10⁹/L = 1 ×10³/µL` (factor 1). ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded. The set is defined by anchoring on the LOINC Group for monocyte number concentration in blood and pulling in all its descendants. This naturally excludes: - **Percentage / fraction variants** — monocytes as a fraction of total leukocytes (`Monocytes/100 leukocytes`, `Monocytes/Leukocytes [Pure number fraction]`) — out of scope, separate measurand. - **Aggregated counts** — monocytes combined with other cell types, e.g. `3005836 / 32349-3 — Basophils+Eosinophils+Monocytes [#/volume] in Blood`, are not monocyte-specific. - **Monocyte subsets** (classical / intermediate / non-classical, defined by CD14/CD16) — out of scope, captured under flow-cytometry concept sets if needed. - **Other specimens**: cord blood, fetal blood, body fluid, bone marrow, CSF, dialysis fluid, peritoneal/pleural/synovial fluid — out of scope for a "blood" concept set. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "Monocytes", "Mono", "MO#" or similar absolute count without specifying the method: `3001604 / 26484-6 — Monocytes [#/volume] in Blood`. By far the most common. - **Method-specific source**: if the source explicitly states "automated", "manual" or "flow cytometry", map to the corresponding method-specific concept. Otherwise, default to `3001604 / 26484-6 — Monocytes [#/volume] in Blood`. - **Source reports a percentage** (e.g. `8 %` of leukocytes): this is **not** the same measurand. It belongs to a different concept set (monocyte percentage). - **Unit notation**: source systems may report `10*9/L`, `G/L`, `10*3/uL`, `K/uL`, or `/uL` (×1000). All correspond to the same physical quantity and can be converted to the recommended `10*9/L` (factor 1 with `10*3/uL`, factor 0.001 with `/uL`). - **Do not confuse with**: monocyte percentage, monocyte subsets (classical/intermediate/non-classical), aggregations like Basophils+Eosinophils+Monocytes, or monocytes on non-blood specimens — all belong to other concept sets. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 26484-6 — Monocytes [#/volume] in Blood (Part description for "Monocytes"). Available: https://loinc.org/26484-6/ [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
